issue: REQ-014 (behavioral-spec.md lines 248-261) prescribes a flat signature `configureVault(ports, userSelectedPath): Result<VaultDirectoryConfigured, VaultConfigError>`. The implementation exposes a curried shape `configureVault(deps): (input: ConfigureVaultInput) => Result<...>` and wraps `userSelectedPath` in an object. Tests adapt to this curried shape, so red-green stays consistent, but the public spec signature now does not match the public implementation signature, and any future caller written to REQ-014 will fail to type-check. None of the carry-forward resolutions FIND-001..FIND-009 authorize this shape change.
remediation: Either flatten the signature to match REQ-014 (`configureVault(deps, { userSelectedPath })` or `configureVault(deps, userSelectedPath)`) or, if curry is intentional, amend REQ-014 in a Phase-1c follow-up so the spec and the impl agree.
